code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triple objects into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # simple verb mapping for common predicates
    verb_map = {
        "city": "is in",
        "country": "is in",
        "latinName": "has Latin name",
        "nickname": "is nicknamed",
        "rector": "has rector",
        "academicStaffSize": "has academic staff of",
        "established": "was established in",
        "state": "is in state",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "hasToItsNortheast": "has to its northeast",
        "dean": "has dean",
        "location": "is located in",
        "numberOfStudents": "has",
        "affiliation": "is affiliated with",
        "director": "has director",
        "motto": "has motto",
        "numberOfPostgraduateStudents": "has",
        "numberOfUndergraduateStudents": "has",
        "officialSchoolColour": "has official colour",
        "outlookRanking": "has outlook ranking",
        "sportsOffered": "offers sport",
        "wasGivenTheTechnicalCampusStatusBy": "was given technical campus status by",
        "founder": "was founded by",
        "leader": "has leader",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "religion": "has religion",
        "headquarter": "has headquarters in",
        "largestCity": "has largest city",
        "river": "has river",
        "hasToItsWest": "has to its west",
        "anthem": "has anthem",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "legislature": "has legislature",
        "sportGoverningBody": "has sport governing body",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "neighboringMunicipality": "neighbors",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "patronSaint": "has patron saint",
        "hasToItsNorthwest": "has to its northwest",
        "campus": "has campus",
        "president": "has president",
        "staff": "has staff",
        "numberOfDoctoralStudents": "has doctoral students",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "has elevation",
        "postalCode": "has postal code",
        "longName": "has long name",
    }

    # helper to clean object strings
    def clean(obj: str) -> str:
        return obj.strip('"')

    # custom phrasing for specific predicates
    custom_phrases = {
        "numberOfStudents": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {clean(o)} students",
        "numberOfPostgraduateStudents": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {clean(o)} postgraduate students",
        "numberOfUndergraduateStudents": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {clean(o)} undergraduate students",
        "academicStaffSize": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an academic staff of {clean(o)}",
        "officialSchoolColour": lambda s, o: f"{s} has official school colour {clean(o)}",
        "outlookRanking": lambda s, o: f"{s} is ranked {clean(o)} in the Outlook ranking",
        "sportsOffered": lambda s, o: f"{s} offers the sport {clean(o)}",
        "hasToItsNortheast": lambda s, o: f"{s} lies to the northeast of {clean(o)}",
        "hasToItsNorthwest": lambda s, o: f"{s} lies to the northwest of {clean(o)}",
        "hasToItsWest": lambda s, o: f"{s} lies to the west of {clean(o)}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {clean(o)}",
        "neighboringMunicipality": lambda s, o: f"{s} neighbours {clean(o)}",
        "campus": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a campus at {clean(o)}",
        "motto": lambda s, o: f"{s} has the motto \"{clean(o)}\"",
        "latinName": lambda s, o: f"{s} has the Latin name \"{clean(o)}\"",
        "nickname": lambda s, o: f"{s} is nicknamed \"{clean(o)}\"",
        "rector": lambda s, o: f"{s} has rector {clean(o)}",
        "dean": lambda s, o: f"{s} has dean {clean(o)}",
        "director": lambda s, o: f"{s} has director {clean(o)}",
        "president": lambda s, o: f"{s} has president {clean(o)}",
        "founder": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded by {clean(o)}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{s} has leader {clean(o)}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"{s} has leader title {clean(o)}",
        "religion": lambda s, o: f"{s} practices {clean(o)}",
        "headquarter": lambda s, o: f"{s} has its headquarters in {clean(o)}",
        "largestCity": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s largest city is {clean(o)}",
        "river": lambda s, o: f"{s} is traversed by the river {clean(o)}",
        "anthem": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s anthem is \"{clean(o)}\"",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"{s} has ethnic group {clean(o)}",
        "legislature": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a legislature called {clean(o)}",
        "sportGoverningBody": lambda s, o: f"{s} is governed by {clean(o)}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s capital is {clean(o)}",
        "patronSaint": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s patron saint is {clean(o)}",
        "postalCode": lambda s, o: f"{s} has postal code {clean(o)}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda s, o: f"{s} lies at an elevation of {clean(o)} meters above sea level",
        "longName": lambda s, o: f"{s} is officially called {clean(o)}",
    }

    clauses = []
    for t in triples:
        # use custom phrasing when available
        if t.predicate in custom_phrases:
            clause = custom_phrases[t.predicate](t.subject, t.object)
        else:
            verb = verb_map.get(t.predicate, None)
            if verb:
                obj = clean(t.object)
                clause = f"{t.subject} {verb} {obj}"
            else:
                clause = f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {t.object}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    # join clauses into one sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."

    # capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
